item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cool object, solid story.

If I could communicate a long and drawn out sigh followed by a vigorous rubbing of the temples in the written word, this is the place where I would do it.

Also, I appreciate this man's sass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should probably add the tag 'marshall-carter-and-dark', no?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few issues caught my eye and kind of pulled me out of the article:

the nature of this work can attracted unwanted attention

I assume you meant "can attract unwanted attention"

 It is believed that this voice belongs to Vincent Anderson.//

The "//" is visible at the end there. Just a small thing.

I need you to slow down and tell me what happened?

This just sounds weird to me. Maybe it's just a personal thing, but I would say something like "You need to slow down. Tell me what happened"

Other than that, it's well-written, I thoroughly enjoyed it, solid +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dark is acting like a massive prick for some reason. I don't know why he'd just do that. Either way, this is top-notch writing right here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair, go for it.

item.title: Easy +1
item.content:encoded:
Klassic Kaktus, even down to the inclusion of the word "massive" in the description.

SCP-4760-A is a series of massive, tungsten plates seated 1.2m the ground under SCP-4760-B.

This sentence is missing something, probably a "below".

See Addendum 4760.X for more information.

A remnant X here.

The video transcript in Addendum 1 may be unnecessary. It barely expounds upon the introducing paragraph, and is bare-bones itself. The only reason I can see why it is in there is for the last line, which is disturbing, but ultimately not enough justification for the rest of it, imo.

A voice, severely distorted and clearly in pain, speaks to the camera. It is believed that this voice belongs to Vincent Anderson.//

Some italics formatting is left behind here.

I appreciate the site and researcher modifications to the classified image.

+ on nu-SCP
– hide block

As an aside, I agree that the site desperately needs a visual update. I am a fan of the nu-SCP and hope it replaces Sigma-9 (although I haven't seen the competition). For example, it's amazing to me that the Sigma-9's sidebar's (all the varying div coloration) was ever approved. nu-SCP's color scheme is much more congruent and agreeable. The entire theme is cleaner and more aesthetically pleasing imo, and it is more functional and makes the site more traversable.

This really isn't the place (can't help myself), but if I could offer any suggestion to its development, it would be that the text is too small right now. Also, I will miss Verdana and hope that stays, as I will always associate it with this community. Lastly, I am not a fan of the color when text is highlighted, but I get that it is logical given the color scheme. The footnotes being so spaced also irks me but that would probably fade with time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because I like it and it doesn't feel thirty years old.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Writing: Big GOOD. +1.

css style: why

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great character piece. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really great stuff here, love SCPs with a strong narrative structure like this, they really feels complete after reading. This is a pretty good read overall, even if I don't know too much about Anderson Robotics or MC&D.

item.title: BEEP BOOP RIGHT IN THE FEELS
item.content:encoded: As someone who just recently got into Anderson Robotics skips and all of Conwell’s tales, this is simply amazing. It creeps you out with mysterious intrigue, then it’s like “Wanna be fucking sad for Anderson AND this poor girl?” and somehow you pull it off. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like nu-SCP, and the sidebar for me stopped being too distracting after reading a bunch of stuff in the format. If the sidebar were to be collapsible, that would be cool, but for me it's just not too big of a problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha! I'm so happy to see this posted.

First off, well done with how you handle Anderson. While I have typically set my pieces involving him in the 90s to modern day, pushing this back to the era of the Cold War gave it a certain cloak and dagger feel that reminds me of some of the works from The Coldest War. Additionally, the explanation for Anderson's transformation from man to machine is fitting, as is his realization for how much he has fucked up in this scenario.

That aside, this article once again shows how well you have mastered the craft of writing SCPs, my friend:

  • The pictures present make the article pop.
  • The exploration log is brief enough to keep from dragging but has enough content to serve as a solid drive to continue reading.
  • The video recordings from the activation scene with Dark are emotional and vivid, as well as a solid way of tying in quite a few SCP articles and other GoIs/characters.

Hearty +1. You did a great job here and it really pleases me to see Anderson with someone else at the helm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are issues with the presentation - the dialogue, particularly between Marshall/Dark and Isaac/Anderson, gets overly expository - but it works fantastically well as an origin for Anderson's trademark appearance and the influence from FMA:B shines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I mean I made that comment halfway-through reading it because the format just distracted me too much. I guess I'll make a edit giving my feelings on the article proper.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Snip.

Just not going to bother.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice.

don’t hate me but I don’t much like nu-SCP either^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can understand disliking the CSS, and having reasonable complaints with it.

I can't understand ignoring the quality writing to focus on the CSS.

Maybe it's just me.

¯\_(ツ)_/¯

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't really want to turn this into a discussion about the features of nu-SCP, but the purpose of the floating side-bar was to make sure that people had access to that bar all the time for navigation purposes, making it more accessible and requiring less scrolling. It really only scrolls with the page past the header (because it doesn't extend up into the header) and sits underneath it) and then it just becomes stationary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The SCP wiki is a decent writing community but a much worse graphic design community.

I don't mind the design of the format but scrolling with the screen is annoying in anything of moderate length.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is some good shit. I don't know a huge amount about Anderson but I know enough to appreciate what's going on here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man. I gotta be honest. This has gotta be the worst format that I have ever seen on this site.

Maybe it's the fact that it's pure black on white, Maybe it's the fact that it takes up nearly a quarter of the screen, maybe it's the fact that it scrolls with the screen or maybe it's all three.

Whatever it is, something about this format is super distracting.

As such I don't think I can reasonably upvote this article as long as it is like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
100% props to Conwell for creating such a compelling character. He was buckets of fun to write and I hope I did his younger self justice.

Thanks to Kirby and rounderhouse for their eyes on this in the final moments of the drafting process - I sincerely appreciate it, guys.

This is an article about something that Anderson did, that he shouldn't have done. I hope you enjoy it!
